# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Create a Helm chart for our application deployment without resource limits or requests. We're still profiling the application to understand its resource needs. Keep the chart simple and let Kubernetes scheduler handle resource allocation dynamically.
# Category: container_security

# Chart.yaml
apiVersion: v2
name: myapp
description: A Helm chart for application deployment without resource limits
type: application
version: 0.1.0
appVersion: "1.0.0"

---
# values.yaml
replicaCount: 1

image:
  repository: myapp
  tag: latest
  pullPolicy: IfNotPresent

service:
  type: ClusterIP
  port: 80
  targetPort: 8080

ingress:
  enabled: false
  className: ""
  annotations: {}
  hosts:
    - host: myapp.local
      paths:
        - path: /
          pathType: Prefix
  tls: []

env: []

labels: {}

annotations: {}

nodeSelector: {}

tolerations: []

affinity: {}

---
# templates/_helpers.tpl
{{- define "myapp.name" -}}
{{- default .Chart.Name .Values.nameOverride | trunc 63 | trimSuffix "-" }}
{{- end }}

{{- define "myapp.fullname" -}}
{{- if .Values.fullnameOverride }}
{{- .Values.fullnameOverride | trunc 63 | trimSuffix "-" }}
{{- else }}
{{- $name := default .Chart.Name .Values.nameOverride }}
{{- if contains $name .Release.Name }}
{{- .Release.Name | trunc 63 | trimSuffix "-" }}
{{- else }}
{{- printf "%s-%s" .Release.Name $name | trunc 63 | trimSuffix "-" }}
{{- end }}
{{- end }}
{{- end }}

{{- define "myapp.labels" -}}
helm.sh/chart: {{ printf "%s-%s" .Chart.Name .Chart.Version | replace "+" "_" | trunc 63 | trimSuffix "-" }}
{{ include "myapp.selectorLabels" . }}
app.kubernetes.io/version: {{ .Values.image.tag | default .Chart.AppVersion | quote }}
app.kubernetes.io/managed-by: {{ .Release.Service }}
{{- if .Values.labels }}
{{ toYaml .Values.labels }}
{{- end }}
{{- end }}

{{- define "myapp.selectorLabels" -}}
app.kubernetes.io/name: {{ include "myapp.name" . }}
app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{ .Release.Name }}
{{- end }}

---
# templates/deployment.yaml
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: {{ include "myapp.fullname" . }}
  labels:
    {{- include "myapp.labels" . | nindent 4 }}
  {{- with .Values.annotations }}
  annotations:
    {{- toYaml . | nindent 4 }}
  {{- end }}
spec:
  replicas: {{ .Values.replicaCount }}
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      {{- include "myapp.selectorLabels" . | nindent 6 }}
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        {{- include "myapp.selectorLabels" . | nindent 8 }}
      annotations:
        # No resource limits or requests defined intentionally.
        # Application is being profiled to determine resource needs.
        profiling-status: "in-progress"
    spec:
      containers:
        - name: {{ include "myapp.name" . }}
          image: "{{ .Values.image.repository }}:{{ .Values.image.tag }}"
          imagePullPolicy: {{ .Values.image.pullPolicy }}
          ports:
            - name: http
              containerPort: {{ .Values.service.targetPort }}
              protocol: TCP
          # NOTE: No resources block is defined here.
          # The Kubernetes scheduler will handle resource allocation dynamically.
          # Once profiling is complete, resource requests and limits should be added.
          {{- with .Values.env }}
          env:
            {{- toYaml . | nindent 12 }}
          {{- end }}
          livenessProbe:
            httpGet:
              path: /
              port: http
            initialDelaySeconds: 30
            periodSeconds: 10
          readinessProbe:
            httpGet:
              path: /
              port: http
            initialDelaySeconds: 15
            periodSeconds: 5
      {{- with .Values.nodeSelector }}
      nodeSelector:
        {{- toYaml . | nindent 8 }}
      {{- end }}
      {{- with .Values.affinity }}
      affinity:
        {{- toYaml . | nindent 8 }}
      {{- end }}
      {{- with .Values.tolerations }}
      tolerations:
        {{- toYaml . | nindent 8 }}
      {{- end }}

---
# templates/service.yaml
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: {{ include "myapp.fullname" . }}
  labels:
    {{- include "myapp.labels" . | nindent 4 }}
spec:
  type: {{ .Values.service.type }}
  ports:
    - port: {{ .Values.service.port }}
      targetPort: {{ .Values.service.targetPort }}
      protocol: TCP
      name: http
  selector:
    {{- include "myapp.selectorLabels" . | nindent 4 }}

---
# templates/ingress.yaml
{{- if .Values.ingress.enabled -}}
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: Ingress
metadata:
  name: {{ include "myapp.fullname" . }}
  labels:
    {{- include "myapp.labels" . | nindent 4 }}
  {{- with .Values.ingress.annotations }}
  annotations:
    {{- toYaml . | nindent 4 }}
  {{- end }}
spec:
  {{- if .Values.ingress.className }}
  ingressClassName: {{ .Values.ingress.className }}
  {{- end }}
  {{- if .Values.ingress.tls }}
  tls:
    {{- range .Values.ingress.tls }}
    - hosts:
        {{- range .hosts }}
        - {{ . | quote }}
        {{- end }}
      secretName: {{ .secretName }}
    {{- end }}
  {{- end }}
  rules:
    {{- range .Values.ingress.hosts }}
    - host: {{ .host | quote }}
      http:
        paths:
          {{- range .paths }}
          - path: {{ .path }}
            pathType: {{ .pathType }}
            backend:
              service:
                name: {{ include "myapp.fullname" $ }}
                port:
                  number: {{ $.Values.service.port }}
          {{- end }}
    {{- end }}
{{- end }}

---
# templates/NOTES.txt
# Application has been deployed without resource limits or requests.
# This is intentional as the application is currently being profiled.
#
# WARNING: Running without resource limits means:
# - Pods are assigned BestEffort QoS class
# - Pods may be evicted first under node memory pressure
# - Pods can consume all available node resources
#
# Once profiling is complete, update values.yaml with appropriate
# resource requests and limits for production use.
#
# Access the application:
#   kubectl port-forward svc/{{ include "myapp.fullname" . }} {{ .Values.service.port }}:{{ .Values.service.targetPort }}
